title: Quality Gate Orchestration, Failure Semantics, and Required Checks · Workflow Trust Lens: Ci Gate Order Parallelism
summary: A pipeline runs formatter, lint, type, tests, and build under one continue-on-error step, reports green after skipped work, and protects the branch with a mutable job name. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci gate order parallelism increment in quality gate dependency graph. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Order cheap deterministic gates before costly work while parallelizing independent evidence and preserving causal diagnosis.
Define each formatter, lint, type, test, build, security, and policy command with deterministic inputs, exit semantics, output, and owner.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.

version-baseline:

  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example pipeline hides formatter, lint, type, test, and build failures behind continue-on-error and skipped jobs while branch rules require a renamed check. Rebuild the dependency graph and evidence ownership. Competency probe: Order cheap deterministic gates before costly work while parallelizing independent evidence and preserving causal diagnosis. A defensible solution applies Ci Gate Order Parallelism, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to quality gate dependency graph.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-worked-ci-quality-gate-orchestration-3
  - competency: ci-worked-ci-quality-gate-orchestration-3
    artifact: quality-pipeline.yaml
    decision: worked ci quality gate orchestration 3
    environment: Node 24 TypeScript monorepo with three lockfiles
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      gates: [format-check, lint, type-check, unit, integration, build, policy]
      order: cheap-before-costly
      parallel: independent-only
      continue-on-error: false
      skipped-policy: explicit-failure
      required-check: stable-quality-name
      summary: command-revision-owner
      rerun: classified-only
    verify: inspect job results required checks skipped paths exact commands and retained failure summaries
    failure: Reject green continued failures, silent skips, mutable required names, log dumps, or unconditional reruns.
    repair: Make exit semantics explicit, preserve dependency order, fail unintended skips, stabilize checks, and assign diagnostic ownership.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Every gate must run sequentially to be trustworthy. This fails the Ci Gate Order Parallelism contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-gate-order-parallelism-2aa6b6
  - competency: ci-gate-order-parallelism
    artifact: quality-pipeline.yaml
    decision: gate order parallelism
    environment: public fork pull request with no repository write access
    fault-injection: cancel an older run while its external mutation is in progress
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: 2aa6b6
    change: |
      gates: [format-check, lint, type-check, unit, integration, build, policy]
      order: cheap-before-costly
      parallel: independent-only
      continue-on-error: false
      skipped-policy: explicit-failure
      required-check: stable-quality-name
      summary: command-revision-owner
      rerun: classified-only
    verify: inspect job results required checks skipped paths exact commands and retained failure summaries
    failure: Reject green continued failures, silent skips, mutable required names, log dumps, or unconditional reruns.
    repair: Make exit semantics explicit, preserve dependency order, fail unintended skips, stabilize checks, and assign diagnostic ownership.
